note: University of the Western Cape (UWC) undergraduate programs organized by faculty. Domain weights (I=Intellectual, C=Cognitive, P=Psychological, B=Behavioral), RIASEC codes (R=Realistic, I=Investigative, A=Artistic, S=Social, E=Enterprising, C=Conventional), and Big Five weights based on vocational psychology literature.

fields.name: Faculty of Natural Sciences
fields.note: Science careers emphasize analytical thinking, problem-solving, and systematic inquiry.
fields.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and traits like {topBigFiveTraits}, you may thrive in Natural Sciences fields that reward analytical problem-solving and systematic inquiry.

roles.name: BSc Biological Sciences
roles.description: Studies living organisms, ecosystems, and biological processes through lab work and field research.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Biological Sciences could suit your curiosity about living systems and analytical approach.

roles.name: BSc Chemistry
roles.description: Explores matter, chemical reactions, and molecular structures through laboratory experimentation.
roles.explanationTemplate: Because of your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits}, Chemistry may match your precision and systematic problem-solving.

roles.name: BSc Physics
roles.description: Investigates fundamental laws of nature, matter, energy, and the physical universe.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits}, Physics could align with your abstract reasoning and theoretical thinking.

roles.name: BSc Mathematics
roles.description: Develops logical reasoning and problem-solving through pure and applied mathematics.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} profile and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Mathematics may suit your logical reasoning and abstract thinking.

roles.name: BSc Computer Science
roles.description: Combines algorithmic thinking, programming, and system design to solve computational problems.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits}, Computer Science could be a strong fit for your structured problem-solving and persistence.

roles.name: BSc Information Systems
roles.description: Integrates technology, business processes, and data management to solve organizational challenges.
roles.explanationTemplate: Because of your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits}, Information Systems may leverage both your analytical and interpersonal skills.

fields.name: Faculty of Economic and Management Sciences
fields.note: Business and economics fields emphasize strategic thinking, leadership, and organizational skills.
fields.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits} indicate strengths in Economic and Management Sciences, especially in leadership and strategic decision-making.

roles.name: BCom Accounting
roles.description: Focuses on financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and management accounting principles.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits}, Accounting may align with your attention to detail and structured approach.

roles.name: BCom Business Management
roles.description: Develops leadership, strategy, and operational management skills for organizational success.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Business Management could suit your leadership and decision-making abilities.

roles.name: BCom Economics
roles.description: Analyzes economic systems, markets, policy, and resource allocation using quantitative methods.
roles.explanationTemplate: Because of your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits}, Economics may match your analytical thinking and interest in systems.

roles.name: BCom Industrial Psychology
roles.description: Applies psychological principles to workplace behavior, organizational development, and human resources.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} profile and {topBigFiveTraits}, Industrial Psychology may leverage your people-focused strengths and analytical skills.

fields.name: Faculty of Education
fields.note: Education careers emphasize empathy, communication, patience, and the ability to inspire learning.
fields.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest you may excel in Education, where communication and empathy are vital.

roles.name: BEd Primary Education
roles.description: Prepares teachers for Foundation and Intermediate Phase (Grades R-6) with focus on child development.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} traits and {topBigFiveTraits},Primary Education may align with your nurturing approach and patience.

roles.name: BEd Secondary Education (Mathematics/Science)
roles.description: Trains educators for Senior Phase (Grades 7-9) with specialization in mathematics or science teaching.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Secondary Education may leverage both your subject expertise and teaching abilities.

roles.name: BEd Educational Psychology
roles.description: Focuses on learning processes, student development, and educational interventions for diverse learners.
roles.explanationTemplate: Because of your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits}, Educational Psychology may suit your reflective and supportive nature.

fields.name: Faculty of Community and Health Sciences
fields.note: Health sciences demand empathy, resilience, technical skills, and a balance of analytical and interpersonal abilities.
fields.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} profile and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest strong potential in Community and Health Sciences, where empathy and problem-solving are crucial.

roles.name: BSc Nursing
roles.description: Provides patient care, health education, and clinical support across healthcare settings.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits}, Nursing may align with your caring nature and resilience under pressure.

roles.name: BA Social Work
roles.description: Supports individuals, families, and communities facing social challenges through advocacy and intervention.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Social Work may suit your compassion and commitment to helping others.

roles.name: BA Psychology
roles.description: Explores human behavior, cognition, emotion, and mental processes through scientific inquiry.
roles.explanationTemplate: Because of your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits}, Psychology may match your curiosity about people and analytical thinking.

roles.name: BSc Physiotherapy
roles.description: Rehabilitates patients through movement, exercise, and manual therapy techniques.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} profile and {topBigFiveTraits}, Physiotherapy could leverage your hands-on approach and people skills.

roles.name: BSc Occupational Therapy
roles.description: Helps individuals develop skills for daily living and working through therapeutic activities.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} traits and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest Occupational Therapy may suit your creative problem-solving and empathy.

fields.name: Faculty of Arts
fields.note: Arts and humanities emphasize critical thinking, creativity, cultural understanding, and communication.
fields.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits} indicate strengths in the Arts, where creative expression and analytical thinking converge.

roles.name: BA English Literature
roles.description: Analyzes texts, develops critical reading skills, and explores literary traditions and cultural contexts.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} orientation and {topBigFiveTraits}, English Literature may align with your love of ideas and expressive thinking.

roles.name: BA History
roles.description: Investigates past events, societies, and cultures through research and critical analysis.
roles.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} strengths and {topBigFiveTraits} suggest History may suit your analytical curiosity and pattern recognition.

fields.name: Faculty of Law
fields.note: Legal studies require analytical reasoning, ethical judgment, persuasive communication, and meticulous attention to detail.
fields.explanationTemplate: Your {dominantRIASEC} profile and {topBigFiveTraits} indicate potential for Law, where structured reasoning and advocacy intersect.

roles.name: LLB (Law)
roles.description: Develops legal reasoning, advocacy, and ethical judgment for careers in law and justice systems.
roles.explanationTemplate: With your {dominantRIASEC} code and {topBigFiveTraits}, Law may leverage your analytical precision and persuasive abilities.
